```text
1	---
2	type: epic
3	status: planning
4	created: 2026-04-06
5	---
6	
7	# EPIC-0014: Target color input UX refinements
8	
9	Users enter a color (e.g., `#FF5733`) into the target picker, but the app extracts only hue + saturation and forces lightness to 50%. The swatch then shows a different hex value, which is confusing. The "H+S at L50" explanation is small and buried inside a popover with no persistent indicator. Moving hue/sat sliders silently invalidates the target without warning.
10	
11	## Goals
12	
13	- Make the L50 normalization obvious with clear before/after display
14	- Show a persistent "target active" indicator outside the popover
15	- Lock hue/sat sliders when a target is set to prevent accidental invalidation
16	- Display the working color in all four formats (hex, rgb, hsl, oklch) inline
17	- Support a "drift" model: unlocking sliders keeps target indicator in a stale state
18	
19	## Scope
20	
21	- **Move target state to Zustand** — target fields (`targetInput`, `targetFormat`, `targetHue`, `targetSat`, `targetLocked`) move from local TargetSwatch state to `paletteStore.ts` so sliders can read lock state
22	- **Hue/sat slider locking** — auto-lock on target set; Lock/LockOpen toggle on each slider (reusing LightnessRange pattern); unlocking allows drift without clearing target
23	- **Persistent target indicator** — swatch trigger shows chromatic ring when target is active, dimmed/dotted when drifted, clear (X) button on hover
24	- **Arrow notation in popover** — replace "Input"/"H+S at L50" with `#FF5733 → #BF4040` and annotation "hue + saturation at L50"
25	- **Inline multi-format display** — new `TargetFormats` component in ControlBar showing hex/rgb/hsl/oklch with click-to-copy when target is active
26	- **SliderBase disabled state** — add `disabled` and `accessory` props for lock icon and pointer-events-none
27	
28	## Tasks
29	
30	- TASK-0053
31	- TASK-0054
32	- TASK-0055
33	- TASK-0056
34	- TASK-0057
35	- TASK-0058
36	
37	## Related
38	
39	- TASK-0046 (done) — initial target color input implementation
40	- NOTE-0013 (archived) — original target color input idea
41	- EPIC-0012 — UX discoverability improvements (related but separate)
42
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
# TASK-0056: Add lock toggle to HueSlider and SaturationSlider

Read `targetLocked`, `targetInput`, and `setTargetLocked` from store. Show Lock/LockOpen icon (Phosphor, matching LightnessRange pattern) when `targetInput !== null`. Pass `disabled={targetLocked}` to SliderBase. Unlocking allows drift — does not clear target. SaturationSlider also disables satMode toggle when locked.

# TASK-0057: Redesign TargetSwatch popover content and persistent indicator

**Popover**: Replace "Input"/"H+S at L50" labels with arrow notation (`#FF5733 → #BF4040`) and `text-4xs` annotation "hue + saturation at L50". Canvas picker clears target before applying new pick.

**Trigger button**: When target active + not drifted: chromatic swatch ring (target hue), hex label at `text-text-secondary`. When drifted: neutral ring, dimmed indicator. Clear (X) button on hover when target active, calls `clearTarget()`.

Derived `targetDrifted` computed as `targetInput !== null && (hue !== targetHue || saturation !== targetSat)`.

## Files

- `app/src/components/controls/TargetSwatch.tsx`

# TASK-0058: Add inline multi-format color display in ControlBar

New `TargetFormats` component renders beside/below the swatch when `targetInput !== null`. Four rows: hex, rgb, hsl, oklch showing current working color at L50. Click-to-copy each row via `useCopyToClipboard`. Compact styling: `text-3xs` labels, `text-xs` mono values. When drifted, values reflect current hue/sat with subtle stale indicator.
```

```text
1	import { useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState } from "react";
2	import chroma from "chroma-js";
3	import * as Popover from "@radix-ui/react-popover";
4	import { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
5	import { parseTargetColor, formatTargetColor } from "../../lib/colors";
6	import type { ColorFormat } from "../../lib/colors";
7	
8	const PICKER_SIZE = 200;
9	
10	function ColorInput({ currentHex, sourceInput, sourceFormat, hue, saturation, onApply }: {
11	  currentHex: string;
12	  sourceInput: string | null;
13	  sourceFormat: ColorFormat | null;
14	  hue: number;
15	  saturation: number;
16	  onApply: (hue: number, saturation: number, input: string, format: ColorFormat) => void;
17	}) {
18	  const [value, setValue] = useState("");
19	  const [isInvalid, setIsInvalid] = useState(false);
20	
21	  const targetFormatted = sourceFormat
22	    ? formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, sourceFormat)
23	    : currentHex;
24	
25	  const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
26	    if (e.key === "Enter") {
27	      const result = parseTargetColor(value);
28	      if (result) {
29	        onApply(result.hue, result.saturation, value.trim(), result.format);
30	        setValue("");
31	        setIsInvalid(false);
32	      } else {
33	        setIsInvalid(true);
34	      }
35	    } else if (e.key === "Escape") {
36	      setValue("");
37	      setIsInvalid(false);
38	      (e.target as HTMLInputElement).blur();
39	    }
40	  };
41	
42	  return (
43	    <div className="mt-2">
44	      <div className="mb-1.5 space-y-0.5">
45	        {sourceInput ? (
46	          <>
47	            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
48	              <span className="text-3xs text-text-faint uppercase tracking-wider">Input</span>
49	              <span className="text-xs font-mono text-text-faint">{sourceInput}</span>
50	            </div>
51	            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
52	              <span className="text-3xs text-text-faint uppercase tracking-wider">H+S at L50</span>
53	              <span className="text-xs font-mono text-text-secondary">{targetFormatted}</span>
54	            </div>
55	          </>
56	        ) : (
57	          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
58	            <span className="text-3xs text-text-faint uppercase tracking-wider">Target</span>
59	            <span className="text-xs font-mono text-text-secondary">{currentHex}</span>
60	          </div>
61	        )}
62	      </div>
63	      <input
64	        type="text"
65	        value={value}
66	        onChange={(e) => { setValue(e.target.value); setIsInvalid(false); }}
67	        onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}
68	        placeholder="#hex, rgb(), hsl(), oklch()"
69	        className={`w-full bg-surface-base border px-2 py-1.5 text-xs font-mono text-text-primary placeholder:text-text-faint outline-none transition-colors ${isInvalid ? "border-red-500" : "border-border-default focus:border-text-faint"}`}
70	      />
71	    </div>
72	  );
73	}
74	
75	export function TargetSwatch() {
76	  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
77	  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
78	  const setHue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setHue);
79	  const setSaturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setSaturation);
80	  const canvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null);
81	  const draggingRef = useRef(false);
82	  const [sourceInput, setSourceInput] = useState<string | null>(null);
83	  const [sourceFormat, setSourceFormat] = useState<ColorFormat | null>(null);
84	  const [sourceHue, setSourceHue] = useState<number | null>(null);
85	  const [sourceSat, setSourceSat] = useState<number | null>(null);
86	
87	  // Clear source when hue/sat diverge from what the input set
88	  const effectiveSourceInput = (
89	    sourceInput && sourceHue === hue && sourceSat === saturation
90	  ) ? sourceInput : null;
91	  const effectiveSourceFormat = effectiveSourceInput ? sourceFormat : null;
92	
93	  const color = `hsl(${hue}, ${saturation * 100}%, 50%)`;
94	  const currentHex = useMemo(() => chroma.hsl(hue, saturation, 0.5).hex(), [hue, saturation]);
95	
96	  const drawPicker = useCallback(
97	    (canvas: HTMLCanvasElement) => {
98	      const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");
99	      if (!ctx) return;
100	      const w = PICKER_SIZE;
101	      const h = PICKER_SIZE;
102	
103	      // Draw hue (x) vs saturation (y) at L=50%
104	      const imageData = ctx.createImageData(w, h);
105	      for (let y = 0; y < h; y++) {
106	        for (let x = 0; x < w; x++) {
107	          const h360 = (x / (w - 1)) * 360;
108	          const s = 1 - y / (h - 1);
109	          // HSL to RGB conversion
110	          const c = (1 - Math.abs(2 * 0.5 - 1)) * s;
111	          const hp = h360 / 60;
112	          const x2 = c * (1 - Math.abs((hp % 2) - 1));
113	          let r1 = 0,
114	            g1 = 0,
115	            b1 = 0;
116	          if (hp < 1) {
117	            r1 = c;
118	            g1 = x2;
119	          } else if (hp < 2) {
120	            r1 = x2;
121	            g1 = c;
122	          } else if (hp < 3) {
123	            g1 = c;
124	            b1 = x2;
125	          } else if (hp < 4) {
126	            g1 = x2;
127	            b1 = c;
128	          } else if (hp < 5) {
129	            r1 = x2;
130	            b1 = c;
131	          } else {
132	            r1 = c;
133	            b1 = x2;
134	          }
135	          const m = 0.5 - c / 2;
136	          const i = (y * w + x) * 4;
137	          imageData.data[i] = Math.round((r1 + m) * 255);
138	          imageData.data[i + 1] = Math.round((g1 + m) * 255);
139	          imageData.data[i + 2] = Math.round((b1 + m) * 255);
140	          imageData.data[i + 3] = 255;
141	        }
142	      }
143	      ctx.putImageData(imageData, 0, 0);
144	
145	      // Draw crosshair at current hue/sat position
146	      const cx = (hue / 360) * (w - 1);
147	      const cy = (1 - saturation) * (h - 1);
148	      ctx.strokeStyle = "white";
149	      ctx.lineWidth = 1.5;
150	      ctx.beginPath();
151	      ctx.arc(cx, cy, 5, 0, Math.PI * 2);
152	      ctx.stroke();
153	      ctx.strokeStyle = "rgba(0,0,0,0.4)";
154	      ctx.lineWidth = 1;
155	      ctx.beginPath();
156	      ctx.arc(cx, cy, 6, 0, Math.PI * 2);
157	      ctx.stroke();
158	    },
159	    [hue, saturation],
160	  );
161	
162	  const handleCanvasRef = useCallback(
163	    (canvas: HTMLCanvasElement | null) => {
164	      canvasRef.current = canvas;
165	      if (canvas) drawPicker(canvas);
166	    },
167	    [drawPicker],
168	  );
169	
170	  const pickFromCoords = useCallback(
171	    (clientX: number, clientY: number) => {
172	      const canvas = canvasRef.current;
173	      if (!canvas) return;
174	      const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
175	      if (
176	        clientX < rect.left ||
177	        clientX > rect.right ||
178	        clientY < rect.top ||
179	        clientY > rect.bottom
180	      ) return;
181	      const x = (clientX - rect.left) / rect.width;
182	      const y = (clientY - rect.top) / rect.height;
183	      setHue(Math.round(x * 360));
184	      setSaturation(parseFloat((1 - y).toFixed(2)));
185	      setSourceInput(null);
186	      setSourceFormat(null);
187	    },
188	    [setHue, setSaturation],
189	  );
190	
191	  const handleMouseDown = useCallback(
192	    (e: React.MouseEvent<HTMLCanvasElement>) => {
193	      e.preventDefault();
194	      draggingRef.current = true;
195	      pickFromCoords(e.clientX, e.clientY);
196	    },
197	    [pickFromCoords],
198	  );
199	
200	  useEffect(() => {
201	    const handleMouseMove = (e: MouseEvent) => {
202	      if (draggingRef.current) pickFromCoords(e.clientX, e.clientY);
203	    };
204	    const handleMouseUp = () => {
205	      draggingRef.current = false;
206	    };
207	    document.addEventListener("mousemove", handleMouseMove);
208	    document.addEventListener("mouseup", handleMouseUp);
209	    return () => {
210	      document.removeEventListener("mousemove", handleMouseMove);
211	      document.removeEventListener("mouseup", handleMouseUp);
212	    };
213	  }, [pickFromCoords]);
214	
215	  return (
216	    <Popover.Root>
217	      <Popover.Trigger asChild>
218	        <button
219	          type="button"
220	          className="flex flex-col items-center gap-1 shrink-0 self-end cursor-pointer group"
221	          aria-label={`Target color: ${currentHex}`}
222	        >
223	          <div
224	            className="w-7 h-7 rounded-full border-2 border-surface-base transition-transform group-hover:scale-110 shadow-[0_0_0_1px_rgba(255,255,255,0.15)]"
225	            style={{ backgroundColor: color }}
226	          />
227	          <span className="text-3xs font-mono text-text-faint group-hover:text-text-faint transition-colors">{currentHex}</span>
228	        </button>
229	      </Popover.Trigger>
230	      <Popover.Portal>
231	        <Popover.Content
232	          sideOffset={8}
233	          className="rounded-none bg-surface-overlay border border-border-elevated shadow-xl p-3 z-[60]"
234	        >
235	          <canvas
236	            ref={handleCanvasRef}
237	            width={PICKER_SIZE}
238	            height={PICKER_SIZE}
239	            className="cursor-crosshair block"
240	            style={{ width: PICKER_SIZE, height: PICKER_SIZE }}
241	            onMouseDown={handleMouseDown}
242	          />
243	          <div className="flex justify-between mt-2 text-sm text-text-faint">
244	            <span>Hue {hue}°</span>
245	            <span>Sat {Math.round(saturation * 100)}%</span>
246	          </div>
247	          <ColorInput
248	            currentHex={currentHex}
249	            sourceInput={effectiveSourceInput}
250	            sourceFormat={effectiveSourceFormat}

254	          />
255	          <Popover.Arrow className="fill-surface-overlay" />
256	        </Popover.Content>
257	      </Popover.Portal>
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-10 06:00:01Z)**_

Here's where the target color work stands.

**Shipped on this branch (TASK-0046, commit 8b4d034):** The target swatch popover lets you paste a color in any of four formats (hex, rgb, hsl, oklch), parses it, and pushes the hue + saturation into the store at L50. The popover shows both the raw input and the format-matched L50 version on two stacked rows (`Input` / `H+S at L50`). State is all local to `TargetSwatch.tsx` — `sourceInput`, `sourceFormat`, `sourceHue`, `sourceSat`. When the user drags a slider and hue/sat diverge from what was entered, the source lines silently disappear. No lock, no persistent indicator, no inline formats.

**The open questions you mentioned are already captured in EPIC-0014** (`board/planning/EPIC-0014_target-color-ux-refinements.md`), with six backlog tasks covering exactly what you described. None of them have been started — the store has no `targetLocked` field, no slider has a lock affordance, and there's no `TargetFormats` component yet. Mapped to your three questions:

**1. Locking sliders so a target isn't accidentally invalidated**
- TASK-0053: lift target state into `paletteStore` (`targetInput`, `targetFormat`, `targetHue`, `targetSat`, `targetLocked`) with `setTarget` / `clearTarget` / `setTargetLocked` actions. Setting a target auto-locks.
- TASK-0055: add `disabled` and `accessory` props to `SliderBase` (opacity-40, pointer-events-none, slot for a lock icon in the header).
- TASK-0056: wire Hue and Saturation sliders to `targetLocked`, showing a Lock/LockOpen toggle (the same pattern `LightnessRange` uses). Unlocking is a *drift* rather than a clear — the target stays set, but the palette has moved off it.

**2. Showing the entered value next to the L50-adjusted value**
- TASK-0057: replace the two-row `Input` / `H+S at L50` layout with arrow notation (`#FF5733 → #BF4040`) and a `text-4xs` annotation explaining "hue + saturation at L50". Also adds the persistent trigger indicator: chromatic ring when a target is active, neutral ring when drifted, clear (X) button on hover. `targetDrifted` derives from `targetInput !== null && (hue !== targetHue || saturation !== targetSat)`.

**3. Showing the palette's working color in multiple formats**
- TASK-0054: `formatAllFormats()` helper in `colors.ts` returning `{ hex, rgb, hsl, oklch }` for a given hue/sat at L50.
- TASK-0058: new `TargetFormats` component rendered in `ControlBar` beside the swatch whenever a target is active. Four compact rows with click-to-copy, subtle stale styling when drifted.

**Suggested order to work through them:** TASK-0053 first (store migration unblocks everything), then TASK-0055 and TASK-0054 in parallel as foundation pieces, then TASK-0056 (slider locks), TASK-0057 (popover + indicator redesign), and TASK-0058 (inline formats) last. That sequence keeps each step shippable on its own.

Want me to start with TASK-0053, or is there any part of the epic you want to rethink before we pick it up? A couple of things worth confirming before I touch code: should unlocking a slider actually preserve the target as "drifted," or would you rather unlocking clear it outright? And should the inline multi-format display live next to the swatch in `ControlBar` as the epic suggests, or somewhere else in the layout?

---

 # EPIC-0014: Target color input UX refinements
 
-Users enter a color (e.g., `#FF5733`) into the target picker, but the app extracts only hue + saturation and forces lightness to 50%. The swatch then shows a different hex value, which is confusing. The "H+S at L50" explanation is small and buried inside a popover with no persistent indicator. Moving hue/sat sliders silently invalidates the target without warning.
+Users enter a color (e.g., `#FF5733`) into the target picker, but the app extracts only hue + saturation and forces lightness to 50%. The swatch then shows a different hex value, which is confusing — users reasonably wonder "I put in this hex value, why am I seeing a different one?" The "H+S at L50" explanation is small and buried inside a popover with no persistent indicator, and the same confusion extends to HSL and OKLCH displays.
 
 ## Goals
 
-- Make the L50 normalization obvious with clear before/after display
-- Show a persistent "target active" indicator outside the popover
-- Lock hue/sat sliders when a target is set to prevent accidental invalidation
-- Display the working color in all four formats (hex, rgb, hsl, oklch) inline
-- Support a "drift" model: unlocking sliders keeps target indicator in a stale state
+- Make the L50 normalization obvious with clear before/after display of the user's input alongside the resulting working color
+- Show a persistent "target active" indicator outside the popover so users can see at a glance that they entered a target
+- Display the working color in all four formats (hex, rgb, hsl, oklch) inline so users can verify the identity and copy any representation
+- Support a "drift" model: when users move hue/sat sliders after entering a target, the indicator goes stale rather than silently vanishing
 
 ## Scope
 
-- **Move target state to Zustand** — target fields (`targetInput`, `targetFormat`, `targetHue`, `targetSat`, `targetLocked`) move from local TargetSwatch state to `paletteStore.ts` so sliders can read lock state
-- **Hue/sat slider locking** — auto-lock on target set; Lock/LockOpen toggle on each slider (reusing LightnessRange pattern); unlocking allows drift without clearing target
-- **Persistent target indicator** — swatch trigger shows chromatic ring when target is active, dimmed/dotted when drifted, clear (X) button on hover
+- **Move target state to Zustand** — target fields (`targetInput`, `targetFormat`, `targetHue`, `targetSat`) move from local TargetSwatch state to `paletteStore.ts` so `ControlBar` can render the inline multi-format display without prop drilling
 - **Arrow notation in popover** — replace "Input"/"H+S at L50" with `#FF5733 → #BF4040` and annotation "hue + saturation at L50"
+- **Persistent target indicator** — swatch trigger shows chromatic ring when target is active, dimmed/dotted when drifted, clear (X) button on hover
 - **Inline multi-format display** — new `TargetFormats` component in ControlBar showing hex/rgb/hsl/oklch with click-to-copy when target is active
-- **SliderBase disabled state** — add `disabled` and `accessory` props for lock icon and pointer-events-none
+- **`formatAllFormats()` utility** — compose existing formatters to return `{ hex, rgb, hsl, oklch }` for a given hue/sat at L50
+
+## Deferred
+
+Slider-locking (TASK-0055, TASK-0056) was originally in scope to prevent accidental target invalidation, but it pre-emptively solves a problem users don't actually report and risks making the controls feel constrained. Dropping it in favor of the clarity-focused work above. Revisit only if real usage shows users losing targets by accident.
 
 ## Tasks
 
 - TASK-0053
 - TASK-0054
-- TASK-0055
-- TASK-0056
 - TASK-0057
 - TASK-0058
+- TASK-0055 *(deferred)*

+Add `targetInput`, `targetFormat`, `targetHue`, and `targetSat` fields to `paletteStore.ts`. Add `setTarget()` (sets all fields + applies hue/sat) and `clearTarget()` (nulls all) actions.  # TASK-0055: Add disabled and accessory props to SliderBase
 
+**Deferred** — only needed to support slider locking (TASK-0056), which is no longer planned. The identity-mismatch confusion EPIC-0014 targets is being solved by clearer in/out display and inline format readouts, not by preventing slider movement. Revisit only if locking comes back into scope.
+

 # TASK-0056: Add lock toggle to HueSlider and SaturationSlider
 
+**Deferred** — pre-emptively locking sliders solves a problem users don't actually have and risks making the controls feel constrained. The real issue EPIC-0014 targets (identity mismatch between input and L50 display) is being addressed by clearer popover presentation and inline multi-format readouts instead. Revisit only if users report accidentally invalidating targets.
+
 Read `targetLocked`, `targetInput`, and `setTargetLocked` from store. Show Lock/LockOpen icon (Phosphor, matching LightnessRange pattern) when `targetInput !== null`. Pass `disabled={targetLocked}` to SliderBase. Unlocking allows drift — does not clear target. SaturationSlider also disables satMode toggle when locked.

```text
1	import chroma from "chroma-js";
2	
3	const WCAG_AA_CONTRAST = 4.5;
4	
5	export function getContrastColor(bgColor: string): string {
6	  const whiteContrast = chroma.contrast("white", bgColor);
7	  return whiteContrast < WCAG_AA_CONTRAST ? "#1d1d1d" : "#ffffff";
8	}
9	
10	export type ColorFormat = "hex" | "hsl" | "rgb" | "oklch" | "named";
11	
12	/**
13	 * Parse a color string and extract hue (0-360), saturation (0-1), and input format.
14	 * Supports hex, rgb(), hsl(), oklch(), and named CSS colors.
15	 */
16	export function parseTargetColor(
17	  input: string,
18	): { hue: number; saturation: number; format: ColorFormat } | null {
19	  const trimmed = input.trim();
20	  if (!trimmed) return null;
21	
22	  try {
23	    // oklch() - chroma.js can't parse this natively
24	    const oklchMatch = trimmed.match(
25	      /oklch\(\s*([\d.]+%?)\s+([\d.]+)\s+([\d.]+)\s*\)/i,
26	    );
27	    if (oklchMatch) {
28	      let l = parseFloat(oklchMatch[1]);
29	      if (oklchMatch[1].endsWith("%")) l /= 100;
30	      const c = parseFloat(oklchMatch[2]);
31	      const h = parseFloat(oklchMatch[3]);
32	      const [hue, sat] = chroma.oklch(l, c, h).hsl();
33	      return {
34	        hue: Math.round(isNaN(hue) ? 0 : hue),
35	        saturation: Math.round((isNaN(sat) ? 0 : sat) * 100) / 100,
36	        format: "oklch",
37	      };
38	    }
39	
40	    // Detect format before parsing
41	    const lower = trimmed.toLowerCase();
42	    let format: ColorFormat = "named";
43	    if (lower.startsWith("#")) format = "hex";
44	    else if (lower.startsWith("hsl")) format = "hsl";
45	    else if (lower.startsWith("rgb")) format = "rgb";
46	
47	    const [hue, sat] = chroma(trimmed).hsl();
48	    return {
49	      hue: Math.round(isNaN(hue) ? 0 : hue),
50	      saturation: Math.round((isNaN(sat) ? 0 : sat) * 100) / 100,
51	      format,
52	    };
53	  } catch {
54	    return null;
55	  }
56	}
57	
58	/**
59	 * Format a color at hue/sat/L=50 in the given format.
60	 */
61	export function formatTargetColor(hue: number, saturation: number, format: ColorFormat): string {
62	  const color = chroma.hsl(hue, saturation, 0.5);
63	  switch (format) {
64	    case "hsl":
65	      return `hsl(${hue},${Math.round(saturation * 100)}%,50%)`;
66	    case "rgb": {
67	      const [r, g, b] = color.rgb();
68	      return `rgb(${r},${g},${b})`;
69	    }
70	    case "oklch":
71	      return toOklchString(color.hex());
72	    default:
73	      return color.hex();
74	  }
75	}
76	
77	export function toOklchString(hex: string): string {
78	  const [l, c, h] = chroma(hex).oklch();
79	  const lR = Math.round(l * 1000) / 1000;
80	  const cR = Math.round((c || 0) * 1000) / 1000;
81	  const hR = Math.round(h || 0);
82	  return `oklch(${lR} ${cR} ${hR})`;
83	}
84
```

```text
12	        .then(() => {
13	          setCopiedLabel(label);
14	          setError(null);
15	          clearTimeout(timerRef.current);
16	          timerRef.current = setTimeout(() => setCopiedLabel(null), timeout);
17	        })
18	        .catch((err: unknown) => {
19	          setCopiedLabel(null);
20	          setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Copy failed");
21	          console.warn("Clipboard write failed:", err);
22	        });
23	    },
24	    [timeout],
25	  );
26	
27	  return { copiedLabel, error, copy };
28	}
29
```
</details></tool-use>

```tsx
import { useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState } from "react";
import chroma from "chroma-js";
import * as Popover from "@radix-ui/react-popover";
import { X } from "@phosphor-icons/react";
import { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
import { parseTargetColor, formatTargetColor } from "../../lib/colors";
import type { ColorFormat } from "../../lib/colors";

const PICKER_SIZE = 200;

function ColorInput({
  currentHex,
  targetInput,
  targetFormat,
  hue,
  saturation,
  onApply,
}: {
  currentHex: string;
  targetInput: string | null;
  targetFormat: ColorFormat | null;
  hue: number;
  saturation: number;
  onApply: (hue: number, saturation: number, input: string, format: ColorFormat) => void;
}) {
  const [value, setValue] = useState("");
  const [isInvalid, setIsInvalid] = useState(false);

  const targetFormatted = targetFormat
    ? formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, targetFormat)
    : currentHex;

  const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
    if (e.key === "Enter") {
      const result = parseTargetColor(value);
      if (result) {
        onApply(result.hue, result.saturation, value.trim(), result.format);
        setValue("");
        setIsInvalid(false);
      } else {
        setIsInvalid(true);
      }
    } else if (e.key === "Escape") {
      setValue("");
      setIsInvalid(false);
      (e.target as HTMLInputElement).blur();
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="mt-2">
      <div className="mb-1.5">
        {targetInput ? (
          <div className="flex flex-col gap-0.5">
            <div className="flex items-baseline gap-1.5 text-xs font-mono">
              <span className="text-text-secondary">{targetInput}</span>
              <span className="text-text-faint">→</span>
              <span className="text-text-primary">{targetFormatted}</span>
            </div>
            <span className="text-4xs text-text-faint uppercase tracking-wider">
              hue + saturation at L50
            </span>
          </div>
        ) : (
          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
            <span className="text-3xs text-text-faint uppercase tracking-wider">Target</span>
            <span className="text-xs font-mono text-text-secondary">{currentHex}</span>
          </div>
        )}
      </div>
      <input
        type="text"
        value={value}
        onChange={(e) => {
          setValue(e.target.value);
          setIsInvalid(false);
        }}
        onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}
        placeholder="#hex, rgb(), hsl(), oklch()"
        className={`w-full bg-surface-base border px-2 py-1.5 text-xs font-mono text-text-primary placeholder:text-text-faint outline-none transition-colors ${isInvalid ? "border-red-500" : "border-border-default focus:border-text-faint"}`}
      />
    </div>
  );
}

export function TargetSwatch() {
  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
  const setHue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setHue);
  const setSaturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setSaturation);
  const targetInput = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetInput);
  const targetFormat = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetFormat);
  const targetHue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetHue);
  const targetSat = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetSat);
  const setTarget = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setTarget);
  const clearTarget = usePaletteStore((s) => s.clearTarget);

  const canvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null);
  const draggingRef = useRef(false);

  const targetActive = targetInput !== null;
  const targetDrifted = targetActive && (hue !== targetHue || saturation !== targetSat);

  const color = `hsl(${hue}, ${saturation * 100}%, 50%)`;
  const currentHex = useMemo(() => chroma.hsl(hue, saturation, 0.5).hex(), [hue, saturation]);
  const ringColor = useMemo(
    () => (targetActive && !targetDrifted && targetHue !== null ? `hsl(${targetHue}, 80%, 55%)` : null),
    [targetActive, targetDrifted, targetHue],
  );

  const drawPicker = useCallback(
    (canvas: HTMLCanvasElement) => {
      const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");
      if (!ctx) return;
      const w = PICKER_SIZE;
      const h = PICKER_SIZE;

      // Draw hue (x) vs saturation (y) at L=50%
      const imageData = ctx.createImageData(w, h);
      for (let y = 0; y < h; y++) {
        for (let x = 0; x < w; x++) {
          const h360 = (x / (w - 1)) * 360;
          const s = 1 - y / (h - 1);
          // HSL to RGB conversion
          const c = (1 - Math.abs(2 * 0.5 - 1)) * s;
          const hp = h360 / 60;
          const x2 = c * (1 - Math.abs((hp % 2) - 1));
          let r1 = 0,
            g1 = 0,
            b1 = 0;
          if (hp < 1) {
            r1 = c;
            g1 = x2;
          } else if (hp < 2) {
            r1 = x2;
            g1 = c;
          } else if (hp < 3) {
            g1 = c;
            b1 = x2;
          } else if (hp < 4) {
            g1 = x2;
            b1 = c;
          } else if (hp < 5) {
            r1 = x2;
            b1 = c;
          } else {
            r1 = c;
            b1 = x2;
          }
          const m = 0.5 - c / 2;
          const i = (y * w + x) * 4;
          imageData.data[i] = Math.round((r1 + m) * 255);
          imageData.data[i + 1] = Math.round((g1 + m) * 255);
          imageData.data[i + 2] = Math.round((b1 + m) * 255);
          imageData.data[i + 3] = 255;
        }
      }
      ctx.putImageData(imageData, 0, 0);

      // Draw crosshair at current hue/sat position
      const cx = (hue / 360) * (w - 1);
      const cy = (1 - saturation) * (h - 1);
      ctx.strokeStyle = "white";
      ctx.lineWidth = 1.5;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.arc(cx, cy, 5, 0, Math.PI * 2);
      ctx.stroke();
      ctx.strokeStyle = "rgba(0,0,0,0.4)";
      ctx.lineWidth = 1;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.arc(cx, cy, 6, 0, Math.PI * 2);
      ctx.stroke();
    },
    [hue, saturation],
  );

  const handleCanvasRef = useCallback(
    (canvas: HTMLCanvasElement | null) => {
      canvasRef.current = canvas;
      if (canvas) drawPicker(canvas);
    },
    [drawPicker],
  );

  const pickFromCoords = useCallback(
    (clientX: number, clientY: number) => {
      const canvas = canvasRef.current;
      if (!canvas) return;
      const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
      if (
        clientX < rect.left ||
        clientX > rect.right ||
        clientY < rect.top ||
        clientY > rect.bottom
      ) return;
      const x = (clientX - rect.left) / rect.width;
      const y = (clientY - rect.top) / rect.height;
      clearTarget();
      setHue(Math.round(x * 360));
      setSaturation(parseFloat((1 - y).toFixed(2)));
    },
    [setHue, setSaturation, clearTarget],
  );

  const handleMouseDown = useCallback(
    (e: React.MouseEvent<HTMLCanvasElement>) => {
      e.preventDefault();
      draggingRef.current = true;
      pickFromCoords(e.clientX, e.clientY);
    },
    [pickFromCoords],
  );

  useEffect(() => {
    const handleMouseMove = (e: MouseEvent) => {
      if (draggingRef.current) pickFromCoords(e.clientX, e.clientY);
    };
    const handleMouseUp = () => {
      draggingRef.current = false;
    };
    document.addEventListener("mousemove", handleMouseMove);
    document.addEventListener("mouseup", handleMouseUp);
    return () => {
      document.removeEventListener("mousemove", handleMouseMove);
      document.removeEventListener("mouseup", handleMouseUp);
    };
  }, [pickFromCoords]);

  return (
    <Popover.Root>
      <div className="relative shrink-0 self-end group">
        <Popover.Trigger asChild>
          <button
            type="button"
            className="flex flex-col items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer"
            aria-label={
              targetActive
                ? `Target color: ${targetInput}${targetDrifted ? " (drifted)" : ""}`
                : `Current color: ${currentHex}`
            }
          >
            <div
              className="w-7 h-7 rounded-full transition-transform group-hover:scale-110"
              style={{
                backgroundColor: color,
                border: ringColor ? `2px solid ${ringColor}` : "2px solid var(--color-surface-base)",
                boxShadow: ringColor
                  ? `0 0 0 1px ${ringColor}`
                  : targetDrifted
                    ? "0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.1), inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)"
                    : "0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.15)",
                opacity: targetDrifted ? 0.6 : 1,
              }}
            />
            <span
              className={`text-3xs font-mono transition-colors ${
                targetActive && !targetDrifted ? "text-text-secondary" : "text-text-faint"
              }`}
            >
              {currentHex}
            </span>
          </button>
        </Popover.Trigger>
        {targetActive && (
          <button
            type="button"
            onClick={clearTarget}
            aria-label="Clear target color"
            className="absolute -top-1 -right-1 w-4 h-4 flex items-center justify-center rounded-full bg-surface-overlay border border-border-elevated text-text-secondary opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 hover:text-text-primary hover:bg-surface-hover transition-opacity z-10"
          >
            <X size={10} weight="bold" />
          </button>
        )}
      </div>
      <Popover.Portal>
        <Popover.Content
          sideOffset={8}
          className="rounded-none bg-surface-overlay border border-border-elevated shadow-xl p-3 z-[60]"
        >
          <canvas
            ref={handleCanvasRef}
            width={PICKER_SIZE}
            height={PICKER_SIZE}
            className="cursor-crosshair block"
            style={{ width: PICKER_SIZE, height: PICKER_SIZE }}
            onMouseDown={handleMouseDown}
          />
          <div className="flex justify-between mt-2 text-sm text-text-faint">
            <span>Hue {hue}°</span>
            <span>Sat {Math.round(saturation * 100)}%</span>
          </div>
          <ColorInput
            currentHex={currentHex}
            targetInput={targetInput}
            targetFormat={targetFormat}
            hue={hue}
            saturation={saturation}
            onApply={setTarget}
          />
          <Popover.Arrow className="fill-surface-overlay" />
        </Popover.Content>
      </Popover.Portal>
    </Popover.Root>
  );
}

```

```tsx
import { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
import { formatAllFormats } from "../../lib/colors";
import { useCopyToClipboard } from "../../hooks/useCopyToClipboard";

const LABELS: Array<{ key: keyof ReturnType<typeof formatAllFormats>; label: string }> = [
  { key: "hex", label: "HEX" },
  { key: "rgb", label: "RGB" },
  { key: "hsl", label: "HSL" },
  { key: "oklch", label: "OKLCH" },
];

export function TargetFormats() {
  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
  const targetInput = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetInput);
  const targetHue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetHue);
  const targetSat = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetSat);
  const { copiedLabel, copy } = useCopyToClipboard();

  if (targetInput === null) return null;

  const drifted = hue !== targetHue || saturation !== targetSat;
  const formats = formatAllFormats(hue, saturation);

  return (
    <div
      className={`flex flex-col gap-px ${drifted ? "opacity-55" : ""}`}
      aria-label="Target color formats"
    >
      {LABELS.map(({ key, label }) => {
        const value = formats[key];
        const isCopied = copiedLabel === label;
        return (
          <button
            key={key}
            type="button"
            onClick={() => copy(value, label)}
            className="flex items-baseline gap-1.5 text-left px-1.5 py-0.5 hover:bg-surface-hover transition-colors cursor-pointer group/fmt"
            aria-label={`Copy ${label} ${value}`}
          >
            <span className="text-3xs text-text-faint uppercase tracking-wider w-8 shrink-0">
              {label}
            </span>
            <span className="text-xs font-mono text-text-secondary group-hover/fmt:text-text-primary truncate tabular-nums">
              {isCopied ? "Copied" : value}
            </span>
          </button>
        );
      })}
    </div>
  );
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-10 08:30:49Z)**_

The layout's wrapping badly because the formats column eats horizontal space and pushes `RANGE` onto its own line. Visually it's also awkward — the formats feel detached from the swatch because they sit in a column the user has to read in isolation. Let me move `TargetFormats` to its own row inside the `ControlBar` section, below the main grid. That keeps the main control row stable and gives the formats dedicated horizontal space.

Also, looking at the screenshot, the arrow notation is working correctly: `#FF5733 → #ff2f00` shows exactly the identity mismatch that this epic is meant to clarify. Good confirmation that piece is landing.

```tsx
import { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
import { formatAllFormats } from "../../lib/colors";
import { useCopyToClipboard } from "../../hooks/useCopyToClipboard";

const LABELS: Array<{ key: keyof ReturnType<typeof formatAllFormats>; label: string }> = [
  { key: "hex", label: "HEX" },
  { key: "rgb", label: "RGB" },
  { key: "hsl", label: "HSL" },
  { key: "oklch", label: "OKLCH" },
];

export function TargetFormats() {
  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
  const targetInput = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetInput);
  const targetHue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetHue);
  const targetSat = usePaletteStore((s) => s.targetSat);
  const { copiedLabel, copy } = useCopyToClipboard();

  if (targetInput === null) return null;

  const drifted = hue !== targetHue || saturation !== targetSat;
  const formats = formatAllFormats(hue, saturation);

  return (
    <div
      className={`mt-3 pt-3 border-t border-border-default flex flex-wrap items-center gap-x-4 gap-y-1 ${drifted ? "opacity-55" : ""}`}
      aria-label="Target color formats"
    >
      <span className="text-3xs uppercase tracking-wider text-text-faint">
        Target{drifted ? " (drifted)" : ""}
      </span>
      {LABELS.map(({ key, label }) => {
        const value = formats[key];
        const isCopied = copiedLabel === label;
        return (
          <button
            key={key}
            type="button"
            onClick={() => copy(value, label)}
            className="flex items-baseline gap-1.5 text-left px-1.5 py-0.5 hover:bg-surface-hover transition-colors cursor-pointer group/fmt"
            aria-label={`Copy ${label} ${value}`}
          >
            <span className="text-3xs text-text-faint uppercase tracking-wider">
              {label}
            </span>
            <span className="text-xs font-mono text-text-secondary group-hover/fmt:text-text-primary tabular-nums">
              {isCopied ? "Copied" : value}
            </span>
          </button>
        );
      })}
    </div>
  );
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-10 08:39:39Z)**_

All four tasks are now implemented. Here is a summary of the state.

**What shipped on this pass:**

- `paletteStore.ts` gained four target fields (`targetInput`, `targetFormat`, `targetHue`, `targetSat`) with `setTarget` and `clearTarget` actions. No `targetLocked` field, as planned.
- `colors.ts` got a `formatAllFormats(hue, saturation)` helper returning `{ hex, rgb, hsl, oklch }` strings at L50.
- `TargetSwatch.tsx` now reads and writes store state instead of keeping local `useState`. The popover uses arrow notation (`#FF5733 → #ff2f00`) with a `text-4xs` "HUE + SATURATION AT L50" annotation. The swatch trigger shows a chromatic ring at the target hue when the target is active and not drifted, neutral ring with dimmed indicator when drifted, and a hover-revealed clear X button that calls `clearTarget`. Picking from the canvas clears the target first so the arrow doesn't get out of sync.
- `TargetFormats.tsx` is a new horizontal row rendered inside `ControlBar` below the main slider grid. When a target is active it shows a "TARGET" label followed by four click-to-copy format chips. When drifted the label becomes "TARGET (DRIFTED)" and the whole row dims to opacity 55. It reads live hue/sat from the store so values update as sliders move.

**Why the formats row moved:** Initial placement stacked the formats next to the swatch, but the 20-ish-character oklch string inflated the swatch column and pushed `RANGE` onto a second row. Moving `TargetFormats` to its own row below the grid (with a subtle top border) keeps the primary controls stable while still visually grouping the formats with the target state.

**Verified visually via Playwright at 1280 and 420 widths:**
- Empty state: no ring, no formats row, `#406abf` hex in faint text.
- After entering `#FF5733`: swatch ring turns chromatic orange, hex label brightens to `#ff2f00`, formats row appears with `TARGET HEX #ff2f00 RGB rgb(255,47,0) HSL hsl(11,100%,50%) OKLCH oklch(0.644 0.243 32)`.
- Popover shows `#FF5733 → #ff2f00` with the L50 annotation.
- After nudging hue: formats row changes to `TARGET (DRIFTED)` and dims, swatch ring neutralizes, values update live.
- Clear X resets everything and the formats row disappears.
- Mobile layout wraps the formats row to two lines without breaking anything.

**Things I deliberately didn't touch:** The browser default focus ring on the trigger button renders as a rounded rectangle around the circular swatch after Escape or after the clear X. Visually a little awkward but it's the standard focusable-button behavior and matches the pre-existing pattern. Happy to polish it later if you want.

Type check, lint, and production build are all green. Dev server is shut down. Board tasks TASK-0053, TASK-0054, TASK-0057, and TASK-0058 are all flipped to `status: done` with `completed: 2026-04-10`. The only remaining EPIC-0014 items are TASK-0055 and TASK-0056, which are the deferred slider-locking tasks. Let me know if you want to take a look in the browser before we commit, or if I should go ahead and commit now.
